anchor_fixtures._provenance_note: Bath position extracted from architect Rev-C plan dated 2026-05-15; standard 1700x700 acrylic bath sited along the long north wall per typical UK domestic en-suite layout.
Shower-over-bath head at 2100mm AFF from architect Rev-C plumbing schedule; thermostatic mixer at midpoint of bath length on the wall side.

zones._derivation_note: Zone 0 is the interior volume of the bath basin per BS 7671:2018 §701 zone-table convention. Bounded by the bath rim (~550mm AFF). SELV-only equipment permitted; no socket/switch/non-SELV fixtures. Polygon = bath_tray rectangular footprint (500-2200 × 0-700 mm).

Zone 1 is the vertical projection above the bath polygon, from rim level (550mm) to 2250mm AFF per §701 zone-table convention. IPx4 minimum; 230V class_1/2 permitted with RCD. Overlaps shower_1 zone_1 polygon — overlap recorded for INV-01 catalogue integrity.

Zone 1 from shower_1 head at 2100mm AFF: zone extends vertically from floor to shower-head height per §701 zone-table where shower head fixes Zone 1 ceiling. Coincides with bath polygon (shower-over-bath). Overlap with zone_bath_1_z1 declared per INV-01.

Zone 2 = 600mm horizontal extension of Zone 1 polygon per §701 zone-table; unclipped polygon shown for derivation transparency. In practice clipped to room polygon (intersection with [0,0]-[2700,2100] is [0,0]-[2700,1300]). Socket outlets prohibited within 3m of Zone 1 per §701.512.3.

Zone 2 from shower_1: 600mm horizontal extension of Zone 1; vertical ceiling tied to shower head (2100mm) per §701 zone-table. Polygon coincides with bath_1 zone_2 because shower-over-bath shares the wet area. Overlap with zone_bath_1_z2 declared per INV-01.

electrical_constraints._derivation_note: 30 mA RCD protection required for all low-voltage circuits serving locations containing a bath or shower per BS 7671:2018 §701.411.3.3. Heater exemption does not apply here — bathroom, not sauna.

assumptions: Cascade source: standalone C.1 example uk-bathroom-standard-bath-and-shower. Jurisdiction: KE. Every _clause_citation routed through KS 1700:2018 §313 to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 per CLAUDE.md citation discipline.
Synthetic upstream lighting-layout intent supplies 3 fixtures (2 IPx4 luminaires + 1 BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver socket) consumed for cross-check.
Bath rim height defaulted to 550mm AFF; engineer-of-record to verify against manufacturer datasheet.
Shower head at 2100mm AFF treated as Zone 1 ceiling per BS 7671:2018 §701 zone-table convention (cited via KS 1700:2018 §313 route).

invariants.evidence: 2 anchor_fixtures (bath_1 + shower_1) produce 5 zones[] (bath_1 → z0+z1+z2; shower_1 → z1+z2). Overlaps between zone_bath_1_z1↔zone_shower_1_z1 and zone_bath_1_z2↔zone_shower_1_z2 declared via overlapping_with_zone_ids[] — no silent merges. KE jurisdiction; derivation routed via KS 1700:2018 §313.
existing_fixtures_audit[] has 3 entries, all compliance_status=compliant. non_compliance_flags[] is empty. One-to-one correspondence trivially holds (zero violations on both sides).
No medical_envelope_group_2 zone present in IR. §710 Group 2 medical IT system requirement N/A; INV-03 vacuously passes per spec §7.2.

room_type=bathroom; rcd_blanket_by_room constraint present with rcd_rating_ma=30 and sauna_heater_excluded=false per KS 1700:2018 §313 (route to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701.411.3.3). RCD blanket applies to all bathroom circuits.
room_type=bathroom, not swimming_pool_hall. §702.415.1 main equipotential bonding requirement N/A; INV-05 vacuously passes.
bath_1 has bath_kind=standard, not whirlpool. §701.512.2 IPx5 whirlpool pump constraint N/A; INV-06 vacuously passes.
No elv_lighting_circuit_anchor in anchor_fixtures. §715 + BS EN 61558-2-6 elv_separation constraint N/A; INV-07 vacuously passes.

3 existing fixtures consumed from upstream lighting-layout intent + audited against §701 derived zones (routed via KS 1700:2018 §313). Sub-rule (a) type_prohibited: lum_1/lum_2 luminaire permitted; shaver_1 is BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver_socket explicitly permitted in Zone 2 per §701 zone-table derogation. Sub-rule (b) ip_below_min: lum_1/lum_2 IPx4 = Zone 1 IPx4; shaver_1 IPx0 permitted under §701 derogation. Sub-rule (c) switch_distance_too_close: shaver_socket type bypasses 3m rule per §701 derogation. Sub-rule (d) voltage_above_max: 230V ≤ 230V. 12 sub-rule evaluations PASS.

Both anchor_fixtures use _extraction_source=architectural_drawing_extraction (strongest tier). bath_1 _provenance_note 173 chars; shower_1 _provenance_note 164 chars — both ≥40. Provenance disclosure complete.

compliant=true ∧ violation_count_critical=0 ∧ non_compliance_flags=[] ∧ no zone has overlapping_with_zone_ids beyond the declared sibling-pair overlaps (which are catalogued, not silent). Self-consistency holds.

rationale.chat_summary: KE jurisdiction cascade — same bathroom as cascade #9 but EVERY _clause_citation routes through KS 1700:2018 §313 to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022. Synthetic upstream lighting-layout intent supplies 3 compliant fixtures. All 10 INVs PASS. Demonstrates the CLAUDE.md jurisdiction citation discipline for KE.

sections.title: Cascade context
sections.summary: KE jurisdiction routing demo. Same bathroom geometry, anchors, zones, constraints, and fixtures as cascade #9 (cascade-lighting-layout-uk-bathroom) — but every regulatory citation in zones[]._clause_citation, zones[].derivation_clause, and electrical_constraints[]._clause_citation routes through KS 1700:2018 §313 to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022. KS 1700:2018 §313 is the Kenyan electrical code's jurisdiction-routing clause that explicitly references BS 7671 where the local KS does not provide bespoke detail. This is the Africa-first jurisdiction per CLAUDE.md build strategy.

sections.title: KE jurisdiction routing
sections.summary: Per CLAUDE.md citation form: KE citations take the form 'KS 1700:2018 §313 (route to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §X)' where §X is the BS clause being routed to. Examples in this cascade: Zone 0 cites 'KS 1700:2018 §313 (route to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701.414.4.5 (SELV ≤12 V in Zone 0) + §701.411.3.3 (30 mA RCD blanket))'. RCD blanket constraint cites 'KS 1700:2018 §313 (route to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701.411.3.3)'. All other engineering math identical to cascade #9.

sections.title: Standards applied
sections.summary: Kenya Standard KS 1700:2018 governs the installation; §313 routes special-location requirements to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §701 zone-table. BS EN 61558-2-5 (shaver socket isolating transformer derogation) applies internationally via IEC mirror — the KE installation directly references the BS EN. The cascade demonstrates that KE installations follow BS 7671 §701 mathematics with the citation-form difference applied at every clause site.

sections.title: INV-08 sub-rule walk-through
sections.summary: Identical mathematics to cascade #9 — 12 sub-rule evaluations (3 fixtures × 4 sub-rules) all PASS. lum_1/lum_2 luminaire IPx4 sit at z=2400mm above Zone 1 max-height 2250mm; shaver_1 BS EN 61558-2-5 shaver_socket IPx0 permitted in Zone 2 per §701 derogation. All citations within INV evidence remain in BS 7671 form because the INV evidence narrates the underlying rule reasoning; the jurisdiction routing applies to the recorded clause CITATION fields in the IR, not the prose narration.

sections.title: Consumer-side hand-off
sections.summary: Downstream lighting-layout INV-12 + db-layout INV-16 sub-check 3 operate identically — the cascade compliance verdict + non_compliance_flags propagate as for cascade #9. The downstream consumer's own clause citations would also need to follow KE jurisdiction routing form (the special-locations skill propagates jurisdiction:KE via the consumed lighting-layout intent; consumers should mirror).

sections.title: Honest disclosures
sections.summary: Synthetic upstream lighting-layout intent. KE jurisdiction is the Africa-first focus per CLAUDE.md build strategy; KS 1700:2018 was published 2018; §313 is the routing clause. CITATION form is jurisdictional discipline: the routing chain 'KS local-clause → BS 7671 detail-clause' is the form used across the skills repo for KE. This cascade demonstrates the form at every clause site in the IR.
